title: selaginella thomensis
intro: Selaginella thomensis is a rare and critically endangered species of spore-producing plant, belonging to the Selaginellaceae family, commonly known as resurrection ferns due to their ability to revive after drying out. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Selaginella thomensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Selaginella thomensis, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
